my_thoughts: They said 'you never gave me any answer'—they're frustrated. Now I'm reframing: speaking is a skill. The parallel to music/sculpture helps them see structure. If they agree ('I do think so'), then I can extend: what about hearing? That's the pivot—hearing is also a skill, not passive reception.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student came seeking answers ('you never gave me any answer'). They're frustrated. Now I'm reframing: speaking is an art, like music. This shifts them from 'why won't you answer?' to 'what makes speaking skillful?' The parallel (music, sculpture) will help them see structure.
visible_response:
Do you think that as there is an art in anything else, so there is also an art in speaking...?

He, then, who by speaking receives benefit himself and is able to benefit others, will speak skillfully: but he who is rather damaged by speaking and does damage to others, will he be unskilled in this art of speaking?
